---
title: "Arkansas Medicaid Policy and Medical Debt Shifts"
author: "Research Pipeline"
date: "2026-05-29"
output: github_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# (Leave this setup block at the very top as it is)
1. Introduction & Methodology
... Some text explaining your project ...

2. Automated Data Pipeline & Transformation
This text introduces your data collection.

#################################################################
# ==========================================
# Step 2A: Fetch Urban Institute Debt Data
# ==========================================
debt <- read_excel('[https://urban-data-catalog.s3.amazonaws.com/drupal-root-live/2024/07/02/changing_med_debt_landscape_state.xlsx](https://urban-data-catalog.s3.amazonaws.com/drupal-root-live/2024/07/02/changing_med_debt_landscape_state.xlsx)')

# Explicitly map safe, standardized column names
colnames(debt) <- c('year', 'fips', 'state', 'share', 'median',
                    'share_white', 'median_white', 'share_nw', 'median_nw',
                    'hospital', 'closure', 'uninsured', 'elderly', 'income')

# Convert text variables safely to numeric attributes
debt <- debt %>% 
  mutate(across(-state, ~ as.numeric(as.character(.)))) %>%  
  mutate(state_name = state, state = fips) %>% 
  select(-fips)

# ==========================================
# Step 2B: Fetch UKCPR National Welfare Panel
# ==========================================
state <- read_excel('[https://ukcpr.uky.edu/sites/default/files/2026-02/ukcpr_national_welfare_data_1980_2024_jan26update.xlsx](https://ukcpr.uky.edu/sites/default/files/2026-02/ukcpr_national_welfare_data_1980_2024_jan26update.xlsx)', sheet = 2)

state <- state %>% 
  select(-state) %>%  
  rename('medicaid' = "Medicaid beneficiaries",  
         'state_unemp' = "Unemployment rate", 
         'state_income' = "Personal income",
         'state_poverty' = "Poverty Rate", 
         'pop' = 'Population', 
         'gdp' = "Gross State Product",
         'state_governor' = "Governor is Democrat (1=Yes)") %>%  
  select(state_name, year, pop, medicaid, contains('state_')) %>%
  filter(year %in% 2011:2023) %>% 
  mutate(state_governor = as.numeric(state_governor),
         log_income = log(state_income), 
         log_pop = log(pop))

# ==========================================
# Step 2C: Process Census Bureau Populations
# ==========================================
pop1 <- read.csv('[https://www2.census.gov/programs-surveys/popest/datasets/2010-2020/intercensal/state/asrh/sc-est2020int-alldata6.csv](https://www2.census.gov/programs-surveys/popest/datasets/2010-2020/intercensal/state/asrh/sc-est2020int-alldata6.csv)')
pop2 <- pop1 # Replicating identical self-join match keys per your source layout
mk <- intersect(colnames(pop1), colnames(pop2))

pop <- pop1 %>% 
  left_join(pop2, by = mk) %>% 
  rename_with(tolower) %>% 
  mutate(fips = state) %>%  
  select(-sumlev, -region, -division, -name, -estimatesbase2010, -estimatesbase2020, -census2020pop)

# Pivot demographic variables into a tidy panel structure
pop <- pop %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("popestimate"), names_to = "year", values_to = "pop") %>%  
  mutate(year = gsub('popestimate', '', year), year = as.numeric(year))

# Calculate stratified demographic denominators
total_pop <- pop %>% 
  filter(sex == 0 & origin == 0) %>%  
  group_by(state, year) %>% 
  summarise(total_pop = sum(pop)) %>% 
  ungroup()

treated <- pop %>% 
  filter(age >= 30 & age < 50 & sex == 0 & origin == 0) %>%
  group_by(state, year) %>% 
  summarise(pop = sum(pop)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  rename('age3049' = 'pop')

young    <- pop %>% filter(age <= 18 & sex == 0 & origin == 0) %>% group_by(state, year) %>% summarise(pop = sum(pop)) %>% ungroup() %>% rename('young' = 'pop')
old      <- pop %>% filter(age >= 65 & sex == 0 & origin == 0) %>% group_by(state, year) %>% summarise(pop = sum(pop)) %>% ungroup() %>% rename('old' = 'pop')
white    <- pop %>% filter(sex == 0 & origin == 1 & race == 1) %>% group_by(state, year) %>% summarise(pop = sum(pop)) %>% ungroup() %>% rename('white' = 'pop')
black    <- pop %>% filter(sex == 0 & origin == 1 & race == 2) %>% group_by(state, year) %>% summarise(pop = sum(pop)) %>% ungroup() %>% rename('black' = 'pop')
hispanic <- pop %>% filter(sex == 0 & origin == 2) %>% group_by(state, year) %>% summarise(pop = sum(pop)) %>% ungroup() %>% rename('hispanic' = 'pop')

# Combine population aggregates
POP <- total_pop %>%  
  left_join(treated,  by = c('state', 'year')) %>%  
  left_join(young,    by = c('state', 'year')) %>%  
  left_join(old,      by = c('state', 'year')) %>%  
  left_join(white,    by = c('state', 'year')) %>%  
  left_join(black,    by = c('state', 'year')) %>%  
  left_join(hispanic, by = c('state', 'year'))

# Calculate structural population shares
POP <- POP %>% 
  mutate(white = white / total_pop, 
         black = black / total_pop, 
         hispanic = hispanic / total_pop,
         pop_share = age3049 / total_pop, 
         young = young / total_pop, 
         old = old / total_pop)

# ==========================================
# Step 2D: Compile Final Analytical Dataset
# ==========================================
data_cleaned <- debt %>% 
  left_join(state, by = c('state_name', 'year')) %>%
  left_join(POP, by = c('state', 'year'))

# Cache the processed object locally for downstream analytical processes
save(data_cleaned, file = 'data.RDa')
#################################################################
